sales.title: Septimius Severus (AD 193-211). AV aureus (20mm, 7.07 gm, 12h). NGC Choice MS S 5/5 - 5/5, Fine Style.  Rome, AD 194-195. L SEPT SEV PERT-AVG IMP IIII, laureate head of Septimius Severus right / P M TR P-II-COS II P P, Jupiter, bare to waist, seated left, Victory right on globe in outstretched right hand, grounded scepter in left. RIC IV.I -, cf. 48 (denarius). Calicó -. Extremely rare. Expertly struck from magnificent, pristine fine style dies on satiny flan.  From the Mirabilis Collection. Ex Heritage Auctions, Auction 3089 (21 January 2021), lot 31064 (MS★ 5/5 - 4/5, Fine Style).  Septimius Severus maintained a strong connection with the god Jupiter as part of his effort to legitimize and strengthen his rule over the Roman Empire. Jupiter, the king of the gods and the protector of the Roman state, symbolized supreme authority and divine justice — qualities Severus sought to embody. This connection was especially important as Severus came from North Africa and needed to establish his legitimacy within Rome’s traditional religious and political framework.  The link between Severus and Jupiter was expressed through art, architecture, and coinage. Severus restored and dedicated temples to Jupiter, most notably the Temple of Jupiter Capitolinus in Rome, underscoring his piety and devotion to the chief Roman god. On coins and official imagery, Jupiter often appeared beside or above Severus, symbolizing divine protection and approval of the emperor’s rule.   HID05401242017  © 2025 Heritage Auctions | All Rights Reserved www.HA.com/TexasAuctioneerLicenseNotice
